tasks.title: Core Systems Architecture Spec

The two accepted proposals define what the game does — this spec defines how the systems that make it work are structured.

tasks.title: Purchase Feedback & Rate Visibility
tasks.overview:
Every button press in the game — buying generators, purchasing upgrades — feels imperceptible. The player can't tell if anything happened.

This has been verified by directly observing the running game at localhost:5173. The economy is working correctly — one selfies generator produces ~6.8 engagement/sec passively, which is meaningful. But the UI is completely silent: no rate indicator, no button feedback, no animation on numbers, no indication that a generator is running after purchase.

The root cause is a visibility problem: the player watches a raw total accumulate with no rate surfaced, so they can never perceive that their rate changed. The numbers are fine. This is a pure UX problem.

Specific gaps observed:
- No rate indicator (e.g. +6.8/sec) anywhere in the UI
- No visual feedback on Buy or Post Content button press
- Stat numbers silently increment with no tick animation
- Generator row looks identical before and after purchase — nothing signals it is running

related_items.purpose: Core game feel target — what the player experience is supposed to be

acceptance_criteria: Spec where and how the current engagement rate (+X/sec) is displayed — it must be prominent enough to read at a glance
Spec how the rate display reacts at the moment of purchase — the player must be able to see the delta without reading a number carefully (e.g. the rate jumps, flashes, or animates)
Spec button feedback on Buy and Post Content — press state, confirmation flash, or equivalent
Spec how a generator row communicates 'this is actively running' after purchase
The target feeling at purchase is: 'Something just changed. I did that. I can see it working.'

open_questions.question: If the UX treatment doesn't resolve the feel problem, the numbers themselves may need tuning — game-designer should revisit the upgrade delta curve after this ships.

tasks.title: Viral burst event signal — engine contract + trigger
tasks.overview:
The Core Game UI (task #29) implements the visual choreography for the 'going viral' peak moment per UX spec §9 — counter acceleration, screen energy, sustained 5–10s cascade. The UX spec §9.1 explicitly states: 'Viral triggers are determined by the game loop, not this spec. The UX assumes the engine signals a viral burst event with source_generator_id, source_platform_id, duration_ms (5000–10000), magnitude (total bonus engagement).' No such signal exists in the current game loop. The UI has a dormant viralEvent hook ready to be driven — this task decides what fires it and how.

Architect needs to answer: (1) what in the simulation triggers a viral burst? (e.g., follower-rate spike threshold, algorithm state × generator combo, random-chance-on-post gated by state), (2) where in the tick pipeline does it emit, (3) how is the signal surfaced to the UI — is it persisted state on GameState, a transient event queue on the driver, or a one-shot callback? Game-designer owns the frequency target (UX spec §9.5 notes 15–40 minutes between virals per active play session; open question owned by game-designer).

Fix: `.affinity-chip.glow { animation: affinity-glow 1.5s ease-in-out infinite; }`

tasks.title: Viral burst visual choreography
tasks.overview:
Implements the full 3-phase visual choreography for the viral burst event on the core game screen. Requires task #50 (viral burst engine) to be complete first — this task wires the UI to the `onViralBurst` driver callback and drives the visual sequence from `GameState.viralBurst.active`.

The choreography has three phases per UX spec §9.2:

Phase 1 — Build (0–1500ms): counter accelerates and shifts to viral gold; source generator row gains pulsing halo; source platform card edges illuminate; background saturation bumps +20%; rising sweep sound begins.

Phase 2 — Peak (1500–end): counter ticks at 3–5× normal rate (real — the engine is producing it); particle burst emits from source generator row drifting toward source platform card (sustained); screen-edge vignette pulses in platform-affinity color at 2s cycle; subtle ±1% zoom pulse on the screen.

Phase 3 — Decay (last 1000–1500ms): tick rate decelerates to baseline; counter color returns to baseline (400ms fade); particles dissipate; edge glow fades; summary badge 'VIRAL +N' appears near counter for 1.5s then fades.

The counter tick-rate drama is the star. All other effects frame it. Per spec §9.3: easing the tick rate across the 3 phases (accelerate → sustain → decelerate) is essential — a flat burst feels cheap.

Note: the edge vignette during viral override uses the source platform's affinity color, not the mood color. This is a state on the same vignette layer as the algorithm mood — they do not stack. See algorithm-mood-visibility.md proposal for the vignette layer architecture.

tasks.title: Prestige cluster (Upgrades + Rebrand buttons) + Phase 1 rebrand modal
tasks.overview:
Replace the current single-button rebrand-corner in GameScreen.tsx (~line 321) with the two-button prestige cluster per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §2.1–2.3. Both buttons are visually grouped (~12px spacing, shared tint/border) and sit bottom-right, visually deferential to the engagement counter and generator list. Before the player reaches the first-rebrand eligibility threshold, both buttons render at 3:1 contrast (not hidden) — seeing them teases the system. Hover tooltips: Upgrades shows current Clout balance (e.g. '42 Clout'); Rebrand shows Clout-on-rebrand preview (e.g. 'Rebrand → +8 Clout') using the existing cloutForRebrand(total_followers) function.

This task also KILLS window.confirm. The existing handleRebrand() uses window.confirm, which violates the project SOP on browser dialogs. Replace it with a modal that renders only Phase 1 of the Rebrand Ceremony (the Threshold Review, per §4.2): a factual account of 'Current followers / Clout earned / New Clout balance' and side-by-side 'What resets' and 'What persists' lists, with Cancel and Continue buttons. Cancel closes the modal and resumes the game loop. Continue calls the existing driver rebrand() method and returns immediately to the main screen (Phases 2–4 land in E4). The game loop MUST pause while the ceremony modal is open (production halts, Algorithm shifts pause) per §4.1.

The Upgrades button opens a Clout Shop modal SHELL for now — header 'Rebrand Upgrades', Clout balance display, × close affordance (plus Esc and backdrop click), but an empty list body ('Upgrades coming soon' or equivalent). The real shop list and purchase flow land in E3. Game loop continues ticking when shop is open (not paused, backdrop ~30% opacity) per §3.1. Keyboard nav: Esc closes both modals, Tab focus-traps while open, Enter confirms primary action.

This chunk must ship a working replacement for window.confirm — no interim state leaves the confirm call in place.

tasks.title: Migrate CloutUpgrade schema to per-level arrays + add missing data entries
tasks.overview:
The CloutUpgrade data model in architecture/core-systems.md was updated to use per-level arrays (`values: float[]`, `lookaheads: int[]`) to support non-uniform effects across levels (e.g. engagement_boost L1=1.5×, L2=2.5×, L3=5×). The current code in client/src/types.ts, client/src/static-data/index.ts, and client/src/game-loop/index.ts still uses the OLD single-scalar schema (`value: number`, `lookahead: number`). This task brings the code up to the architecture spec and populates the full upgrade + post-prestige generator data set per the accepted clout-upgrade-menu.md proposal. This is a pure backend/data task — no player-facing UI changes. It is a prerequisite for E3 (the Clout Shop UI) to correctly display and consume per-level effects.

Scope:

1) Type migration in client/src/types.ts:
   - { type: 'engagement_multiplier'; value: number } → { type: 'engagement_multiplier'; values: number[] }
   - { type: 'algorithm_insight'; lookahead: number } → { type: 'algorithm_insight'; lookaheads: number[] }
   - Keep generator_unlock and platform_headstart as-is (max_level: 1)

2) Update static data entries in client/src/static-data/index.ts:
   - Add `engagement_boost`: cost [10, 30, 75], max_level 3, values [1.5, 2.5, 5.0]
   - Update `algorithm_insight`: cost [15, 40], max_level 2, lookaheads [1, 2]
   - Update `platform_headstart_instasham`: cost [20]
   - Update `platform_headstart_grindset`: cost [50]
   - Remove `platform_headstart_chirper` if present (chirper is starter platform)
   - Add `ai_slop_unlock`: cost [25], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'ai_slop' }
   - Add `deepfakes_unlock`: cost [60], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'deepfakes' }
   - Add `algorithmic_prophecy_unlock`: cost [100], effect { type: 'generator_unlock', generator_id: 'algorithmic_prophecy' }

3) Add post-prestige generator definitions to generator static data:
   - `ai_slop`: base_engagement_rate = 8× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.6, trend_sensitivity 0.1
   - `deepfakes`: base_engagement_rate = 15× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.3, trend_sensitivity 0.95
   - `algorithmic_prophecy`: base_engagement_rate = 40× Viral Stunts, follower_conversion_rate 0.5, trend_sensitivity 0.5
   - These generators MUST have NO entry in unlockThresholds.generators (never follower-unlocked; unlocked only via Clout upgrade)

4) Update game-loop effect consumption (client/src/game-loop/index.ts) to index into arrays:
   - engagement_multiplier: cloutBonus reads `effect.values[level - 1]` instead of scalar.
   - algorithm_insight: prestige/index.ts getUpcomingShifts reads `effect.lookaheads[level - 1]`

5) Verify applyRebrand re-applies `generator_unlock` effects after resetting generators to owned:false, per architecture §Prestige post-reset Clout re-application order. Fix if missing. Same for platform_headstart on platform reset.

6) Update tests in client/src/game-loop/index.test.ts, client/src/prestige/index.test.ts, client/src/driver/index.test.ts to match new schema. Add test coverage for post-prestige generator unlocking via applyRebrand.

tasks.title: Clout Shop modal — full upgrade list + purchase flow + states
tasks.overview:
Replace the Clout Shop modal shell from E1 with the full shop per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §3. The shop is ~720×560px, centered, dark-backdrop-at-30%, and MUST NOT pause the game loop — the main screen continues ticking at the modal edges. This is planning, not escape.

Scope:

1) Clout balance header (§3.2): 'Clout: N' top of modal body, 36px/weight-600 numeral, P0 contrast (15.8:1), animates downward (400ms tick) on purchase.

2) Upgrade categories (§3.4), in fixed order: ENGAGEMENT (engagement_multiplier effects), UNLOCKS (generator_unlock + platform_headstart effects merged), INSIGHT (algorithm_insight effect). Within each category, rows sort by ascending cost so next-affordable is near category top.

3) Upgrade row anatomy (§3.3): 72px row height, 28px icon, 16px/weight-500 name, 12px muted-7:1 description (always shown, not hover), 'Lv N/M' / 'owned' / 'locked' state, cost (or MAX or ✓), right-aligned ⬆ purchase button.

5) Insufficient-Clout state (§3.5): button at 3:1 contrast (not faded), cost label amber (not red), on tap: 4px horizontal shake for 200ms, no state change, no sound.

6) Shop states (§3.6): empty (first visit, Clout:0, all rows locked, header copy 'Earn Clout by rebranding. Your first rebrand is ahead.'), partial (default), all-maxed (footer 'You\'ve mastered the system. Rebrand to reset the stage.').

Gate by same Reduce Motion detection used in core-game-screen.md §12.

8) Accessibility: CVD-safe (no color-only signals — text labels ✓/MAX/locked/cost/glyph pair with state), P0/P1/P2 contrasts per role standards, ⬆ button ≥44×44px hit area, full keyboard nav, focus trap while modal open.

tasks.title: Rebrand Ceremony — Phases 2 (Eulogy), 3 (Commit), 4 (Dissolution→Rebirth)
tasks.overview:
Extend E1's minimal Phase 1 modal into the full 4-phase Rebrand Ceremony per ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §4.3–4.6. Total runtime ~8s first rebrand (unskipped), ~5s subsequent (eulogy skipped). The ceremony must feel earned — it fires rarely and deserves its runtime. Game loop stays paused for the entire ceremony (established in E1).

Scope:

1) Phase 2 Eulogy (§4.3, ~3–4s, skippable): 3–4 sequential stanzas, each fading in over 600ms and lingering ~1s. Content sources: top platform by followers, top generator by lifetime engagement, longest-held Algorithm state during run, total engagement earned. Stanzas pulled from a data-driven template library with run-state substitution (see open question). Background desaturates 0% → 40% over 3s (linear). Skip link bottom-right: first rebrand (rebrand_count === 0 → 1) HIDES skip until 3rd stanza appears; subsequent rebrands show skip immediately. Tone: affectionate, deadpan, satirical. Auto-advances to Phase 3 1s after final stanza — no manual advance.

2) Phase 3 Commit (§4.4, ~1s): single headline 'Become someone new.' (24px/weight-500), one 280×80px button labeled 'Rebrand' (biggest button in the game). No Cancel button. Backdrop-click/Esc cancels. On tap: button scale 0.96 (80ms), low-register tone (lowest in game), transition to Phase 4.

3) Phase 4 Dissolution → Rebirth (§4.5, ~3.8s):
   - Dissolution (0–2s): backdrop counters (engagement, followers, generators) tick downward rapidly (0→1000ms ease-in), background desaturates to 10% (500→2000ms ease-in), descending-pitch whoosh sound (800→1800ms), screen fades to deep-muted-blue wash (1500→2000ms ease-in).
   - Rebirth (2–3.8s): 400ms held silence on dark wash, single clean opening tone (higher register than commit tone, 2400→2600ms), fade to fresh main screen over 900ms (2600→3500ms ease-out).
   - Phase 4 has NO cancellation — rebrand is committed to state when Phase 4 begins; visual is just playing out.

4) Cancellation behavior (§4.6): Phase 2 backdrop-click/Esc cancels (no visible Cancel). Phase 3 backdrop-click/Esc cancels (no visible Cancel). Phase 4 cannot cancel.

5) Reduce Motion variants (§7): replace counter tick-down dissolution with single 1s fade-to-zero, replace Phase 2 background desaturation with instant, replace Phase 4 desaturation with instant, preserve sound design AND held-silence timing AND fade-back timing (timing is not motion).

6) Screen reader support (§7): Phase 2 stanzas render with aria-live='polite'. Phase 4 announces 'Rebranding. Run N beginning.' via aria-live.

7) Call driver.rebrand() at the Commit tap (transition from Phase 3 to Phase 4) — this commits the rebrand to state. Phase 4 visual plays out after state is committed.

tasks.title: Remove post-prestige generators from main generator list
tasks.overview:
Post-prestige generators (ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy) are currently rendered in the main generator list in GameScreen, below Viral Stunts, each showing a — x1.00 multiplier and a 'Buy' action priced in engagement (e.g. 'Buy AI Slop — cost: 200.00M engagement'). This contradicts the accepted spec ux/prestige-rebrand-screen.md §3.1 and §3.4, which places these generators in the Clout Shop modal under the UNLOCKS category, priced in Clout, purchased via the Upgrades button in the prestige cluster (bottom-right).

The Clout Shop is a separate planned task (see the existing 'Clout Shop modal — full upgrade list + purchase flow + states' build task). Until it lands, post-prestige generators should simply NOT appear in the main generator list at all. When the Clout Shop ships, they will render there as UNLOCKS rows per spec.

This is a surgical filter change: the generator list rendering logic should exclude any generator whose id is in the postPrestige set (already defined in save/index.ts as ['ai_slop', 'deepfakes', 'algorithmic_prophecy']).

The three symptoms this fixes (all currently visible on screen):
1. The locked state reads as broken — '— x1.00' is ambiguous between 'no multiplier' and 'row failed to load'
2. No PRESTIGE band label — these rows sit below the LATE band in visual no-man's-land
3. Cost is denominated in engagement without any engagement-pool indicator visible on the main screen, so the player has no way to reason about proximity to unlock

All three evaporate when the generators stop rendering here. They will re-emerge in the correct surface (Clout Shop, in Clout) when that task ships.

tasks.title: Creator Kit purchase handler
tasks.overview: Implement the kit-purchase action. The handler validates the purchase, spends engagement, and increments the player's level for that item. It MUST expose a post-purchase hook (or otherwise be extensible) so E6 (Phone mid-run head-start) can cleanly layer in without rewriting the handler. Steps per the architecture spec §Purchase: (1) validate player.creator_kit[item_id] < KitItemDef.max_level, (2) validate player.engagement >= KitItemDef.cost[current_level], (3) decrement engagement, (4) increment level. Reject the action if either validation fails; do not mutate state. Follow the existing UI→game-loop action dispatch pattern used for Clout purchases.

tasks.title: Generator level growth curves: cost parity + L=10 cap + runtime clamps + MAX UI
tasks.overview:
Implement the single coherent correctness fix from the accepted generator-level-growth-curves proposal. Restores cost/reward parity (cost now uses levelMultiplier, not 4^(L-1)), installs a hard L=10 cap per generator, adds runtime clamps that prevent the save-breaking Infinity bug from ever re-emerging, and swaps the Lvl↑ affordance for a MAX label at cap. All changes ship together per architect review — cost parity without the cap still overflows at L~72, and the runtime clamps are permanent invariants that belong next to the formula they guard.

Scope of change (with file:line anchors):

1. DATA CONTRACT — Add `max_level: number` to `GeneratorDef` in client/src/types.ts:358 (mirror the shape of `CloutUpgradeDef.max_level` at types.ts:412). Update the stale doc comment at types.ts:390 that still encodes the old `4^(currentLevel-1)` formula. Seed `max_level: 10` on every generator entry in client/src/static-data/index.ts (10 generators total — selfies, memes, tiktok_dances, podcasts, youtube_shorts, onlyfans, viral_stunts, ai_slop, deepfakes, algorithmic_prophecy). Also update the header comment at static-data/index.ts:33 that encodes the old cost formula.

2. COST FORMULA — Swap `generatorUpgradeCost` at client/src/generator/index.ts:59 to `ceil(base_upgrade_cost × levelMultiplier(targetLevel))` where `targetLevel = currentLevel + 1`. Update the function doc comment. Add at-cap guard: throw (or return a sentinel the callers already expect) when `currentLevel >= def.max_level`. Add the same at-cap guard to `upgradeGenerator` at generator/index.ts:166.

3. RUNTIME CLAMPS (permanent invariants — keep regardless of migration) —
   a) In client/src/game-loop/index.ts:78, clamp `level` input to `levelMultiplier` to [1, 20] before the Math.pow call. Do NOT throw when level > 20 — silently clamp, because this is belt-and-braces for corrupted saves / future code paths that forget a cap.
   b) Clamp `player.engagement` to `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` on every write site. Known sites: game-loop/index.ts:358, game-loop/index.ts:427, game-loop/index.ts:631, model/index.ts:189 (addEngagement), offline/index.ts:203. Simplest shape: a tiny `clampEngagement(value: number): number` helper in model/ that every write routes through.

4. UI — `MAX` label at cap: when `g.level >= def.max_level`, the Lvl↑ affordance in client/src/ui/GeneratorList.tsx (around line 340) shows `MAX` instead of the upgrade cost, and tapping it is a no-op (or the drawer opens but the upgrade button inside UpgradeDrawer.tsx is disabled with a MAX label). Match the visual treatment of Clout upgrade MAX state if one exists in CloutShopModal.tsx — check that file for the established pattern before picking a shape.

Non-goals: do NOT retune base_upgrade_cost seed values (game-designer has filed task #88 for the balance pass). Do NOT add a player-facing 'your save was repaired' notice — game-designer explicitly called for silent clamps. Do NOT raise the cap above 10 (engineer review established that L=14 overflows under the full multiplier stack).

tasks.title: Save migration V4→V5: silent clamp engagement + per-generator level
tasks.overview:
Add a one-time V4→V5 save migration that repairs saves broken by the pre-fix overflow bug. The migration silently clamps `player.engagement` to Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER and each `generators[id].level` to max_level (10). Depends on E1 because the migration reads the new max_level field from the static-data contract E1 introduces.

Per architect review: treat the runtime clamps (shipped in E1) and this schema migration as two separate mechanisms. Runtime clamps are permanent invariants in the hot path. This migration is a one-time data-repair step at a known schema-version boundary. Both stay.

Per game-designer final review: silent clamp only — NO player-facing 'your save was repaired' modal. The Infinity state and over-cap levels were a bug, not earned progress; at that magnitude floats had already rounded to even. A console.warn in the migration function for diagnostic trace is desired; a UI notice is not.

Implementation shape (per architect review):
- Bump CURRENT_VERSION in client/src/save/index.ts:17 from 4 to 5
- Add `migrateV4toV5(data: SaveData): SaveData` following the existing pattern of migrateV3toV4 at line 178
- Append to the migrate() chain at line 225 (matches existing V1→V2→V3→V4 shape)
- Emit console.warn when either clamp triggers, including the before-value for diagnostic trace
- Add tests alongside existing migration tests in client/src/save/index.test.ts
